steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Apply the arc length formula
steps.content:
The arc length formula relates the arc length, central angle, and circumference:
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{\text{central angle}}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$
Substituting the given values (arc length = 4 inches, central angle = 20°):
$$4 = \frac{20^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times C$$

steps.title: Simplify and solve for circumference
steps.content:
Simplify the fraction: $\frac{20}{360} = \frac{1}{18}$
$$4 = \frac{1}{18} \times C$$
Multiply both sides by 18:
$$C = 4 \times 18 = 72$$
That confirms D is correct.
